name: Feedback Form & Survey Questions
text_content:
# Floor Plan Visuals Mobile App Design — Client Feedback & Discovery Survey

This task represents the discovery survey and feedback form prepared to align on branding, design directions, user feedback, and operational priorities. Please review and respond directly to the following questions:

### 1. Brand Assets & Guidelines
Please share your current brand assets/documents, including the currently used wordmark/logo, emblem/symbol, colours, typography, or any existing brand-related materials.

### 2. Branding Rework & Scope Limitations
Before starting the main UX/UI production process, I believe we should first review and rework the currently used wordmark/logo and emblem structure in order to establish a stronger and more consistent visual foundation for the redesign. Do you have any concerns, limitations, or preferences regarding how far the current branding can be updated, modernized, or visually expanded?

### 3. Visual & Aesthetic Preferences
Do you currently have any specific visual style or design direction in mind regarding the redesign or are there any apps, platforms, products, or digital experiences that you personally like, admire, or would want the redesign direction to feel closer to in terms of quality, usability, atmosphere, or overall presentation?

### 4. Competitor Analysis
Could you please share your current competitors in order of importance from your perspective?

### 5. Existing Customer Feedback & Issues
If you currently maintain any list of customer feedback, complaints, recurring requests, or app-related issues collected from users, please share them if possible.

### 6. Team Feedback & Operational Observations
Beyond the previously discussed needs and requests, I would appreciate it if you could internally discuss the app together with your full team and share any additional thoughts, concerns, priorities, or operational observations you collectively have regarding the current experience.

### 7. Critical Sections & Operational Workflows
Which sections, workflows, or operational areas of the app do you consider the most critical, valuable, or important from both business and user perspectives? If possible, please also prioritize them in order of importance.

### 8. Project Reviewers & Approvers
Who will primarily be responsible for reviewing and approving design decisions throughout the project?

name: 5.5.2.A.2 - Service Detail Page Template Layout Design
text_content:
Design and refinement of individual service-detail pages used to present specific services, deliverables, use cases, and supporting informational content.
The current detail pages appear highly repetitive and visually limited despite representing highly important operational and commercial offerings of the platform. 

During the redesign process, these pages may evolve into richer presentation experiences supported by stronger layout structures, contextual visuals, production examples, testimonials, previous project references, or supporting media depending on the selected design direction.
Particular attention may be given to helping users more quickly understand:
what the service provides,
when it should be used,
what deliverables are included,
and why the service may be valuable for their specific workflow or property type.

If it would not create any marketing or operational concerns for your side, pricing information related to each service should ideally also be accessible directly within the corresponding service pages in order to provide clearer expectations and support more informed decision-making throughout the user journey.

The goal will be creating a clearer, more premium, and more confidence-building service presentation experience throughout the platform.

name: 5.5.2.A.1 - Service Category Main Page Template Layout Design
text_content:
Design and restructuring of service-category pages used to organize and present grouped service types such as Marketable, As-Built, 3D & Media, and Add-Ons.
These pages will likely require clearer visual hierarchy, stronger category differentiation, and more engaging browsing structures in order to improve service discovery and help users better understand the scope of available offerings. Depending on the selected design direction, these areas may also benefit from richer visual support such as custom illustrations, photography, visual previews, featured examples, or contextual media elements to create a more premium and informative browsing experience.

Additional attention may also be given to improving:
service grouping clarity,
category navigation,
contextual guidance,
and overall service exploration usability.

name: 5.5.2 - Services
text_content:
During the estimation process, it became very clear that this area represents one of the most commercially valuable and visually important sections of the entire platform. However, the current structure feels relatively minimal and visually repetitive despite the importance of the services being presented. Many valuable offerings currently appear inside highly similar layouts, which reduces differentiation, perceived value, and overall presentation quality.

This area may benefit from stronger visual hierarchy, clearer service positioning, more premium presentation structures, and potentially broader visibility within the app ecosystem rather than existing only as a secondary section accessible through the About area.

name: 5.5.1 - About Main Screen
text_content:
Redesign and restructuring of the main About experience as a clearer and more useful informational entry point for users. This screen may function not only as a traditional company information page, but also as a lightweight operational guidance and service discovery layer within the app ecosystem.

The current structure and content organization will be re-evaluated from a UX perspective. Certain sections — particularly Services, pricing-related information, production details, and operational guidance — may benefit from becoming more contextually connected to related workflows or more visible within the primary navigation structure instead of remaining only inside the About section.

For example, users creating new orders may naturally expect to understand:
available services,
deliverable options,
optional add-ons,
and approximate pricing structures
directly within related operational flows. Because of this, informational content hierarchy and contextual visibility may continue evolving throughout the redesign process depending on usability findings and workflow requirements.

name: 5.5 - About, Catalogs, Pricing & Services
text_content:
Design and restructuring of the informational, service discovery, pricing, and support-related areas throughout the app ecosystem. This phase focuses on improving how users understand available services, production capabilities, pricing structures, operational workflows, and platform-related information during their overall app experience.

Rather than functioning only as isolated informational pages, these areas may evolve into more contextually integrated product-support layers connected directly to operational workflows such as order creation, service selection, onboarding, and customer support experiences.

Throughout this process, the positioning, hierarchy, visibility, and accessibility of informational content may be reconsidered depending on usability findings, workflow requirements, and overall product direction. Your answers to the questions I will be asking, along with additional operational notes and feedback from your side, will also play an important role in defining the most effective structure for these areas.

The overall architecture of this section may continue evolving throughout the redesign process as user behaviors, workflow scenarios, and product priorities become clearer.

name: 5.4.1 - New Order Selection Main Screen
text_content:
During the estimation and structural evaluation process, it became increasingly clear that the current order creation experience would benefit from a more functionally separated and user-friendly grouping structure. For this reason, the redesigned Create New Order experience will likely present workflows under two primary categories: creating new production orders from scratch, and working with existing property-related assets already available within the FPV ecosystem.

This approach aims to reduce complexity, improve clarity, and help users navigate operationally dense workflows in a more intuitive and manageable way. Additionally, when users begin creating a completely new order, the system may also benefit from informing or warning them if an existing plan, survey, or related property asset already exists within the inventory system based on the provided property information.

name: 5.4 - Create New Order
text_content:
This area represents one of the most critical parts of the overall product experience. The order creation process directly affects usability, operational clarity, user confidence, and the overall perception of the platform.

My primary focus during the redesign of this area will be helping users complete what is currently a highly step-heavy and operationally dense workflow in a clearer, more comfortable, and more manageable way.

The current content and available flows were used as the primary reference during the time estimation process. Based on the existing app structure, several order categories and workflow branches appear to be incomplete, placeholder-based, or not yet fully implemented. Since these flows represent core operational services of the platform, they may require additional UX planning, new screen structures, interaction patterns, and dedicated workflow solutions throughout the redesign process.

Due to the complexity and operational importance of this area, certain sections may require additional UX exploration, alternative structural solutions, iterative testing, and multiple refinement cycles in order to achieve the most effective workflow structure. The answers to the questions that will be asked throughout this process, along with additional notes and feedback from both you and your customers, will be especially critical for this area.

name: 5.3.3 - AutoFill Preferences
text_content:
Although this area may initially appear as a simple settings or form screen, it actually represents a highly operational and workflow-critical preference system directly connected to future order creation flows.

The current structure contains a large amount of configuration-heavy content, repeated operational decisions, deliverable preferences, formatting logic, conditional options, and production-related defaults that may significantly affect the usability and efficiency of future order workflows.

Because of this, the redesign process for this area will likely require careful UX simplification, content grouping, hierarchy restructuring, progressive disclosure solutions, and improved interaction patterns in order to reduce cognitive load and create a more manageable and user-friendly experience.

Particular attention may also be required for:
content density reduction
smarter grouping structures
reusable preference logic
expandable sections
onboarding clarity
conditional visibility patterns
repeat-order usability improvements

To improve workflow efficiency and reduce repetitive manual input, larger form-based and operational data-entry areas throughout the app may also benefit from contextual “Save as AutoFill Preference” actions. This would allow users — particularly real estate professionals handling repeated order workflows — to quickly reuse previously entered information, preferences, and operational settings across future orders and related production flows.

The overall structure and operational behavior of this area may continue evolving throughout the redesign process depending on workflow findings and real usage requirements.

Depending on usability and workflow needs, notification grouping structures may also be explored for areas such as operational alerts, scheduling updates, invoice-related notifications, project completion messages, and other workflow-related system events.

This task may also include read/unread states, notification hierarchy, interaction clarity, and overall notification usability patterns throughout the app experience.

The importance level, structural complexity, and overall direction of this module may further evolve depending on the survey responses, workflow priorities, and operational feedback collected throughout the redesign process. At the current stage, the existing notification structure is being used as the primary reference foundation.

name: Project Scope, Unknowns & Workflow Evolution Note
text_content:
Throughout the estimation and structural review process, several areas of the current app appeared partially undefined, incomplete, placeholder-based, or operationally unclear. Because of this, certain workflows, screens, interaction structures, or service-related requirements may naturally evolve further once the redesign process begins and deeper product discussions take place.

Additionally, as the project progresses, new operational needs, structural changes, feature additions, workflow expansions, or more radical UX/UI direction changes may also emerge based on ongoing discoveries, collaborative discussions, and evolving product goals.

Whenever possible, I will do my best to help accommodate and refine these areas within the existing project structure and allocated workflow. However, if certain requests evolve into substantially larger production tasks, new feature systems, or major structural expansions beyond the currently evaluated scope, additional estimation and scope adjustments may naturally become necessary later in the process.

The current estimations are therefore based on the presently visible app structure, available screens, existing workflows, and the information accessible during the evaluation stage.

name: Iterative Review, Validation & Prototype Support
text_content:
PHASE 6 — Iterative Review, Validation & Prototype Support
Continuous collaborative review, revision, validation, and prototype support processes throughout the redesign workflow.

This phase represents the ongoing feedback and refinement layer of the project rather than a single presentation milestone. Throughout production, completed screens and workflows will be continuously reviewed together through Figma-based discussions, annotations, revisions, and iterative feedback cycles until the desired quality and usability direction are reached.

Certain areas may also require partial interactive prototypes to better communicate navigation behavior, interaction logic, and workflow structures during the redesign process.

Due to the highly iterative and exploratory nature of these tasks, scope and required effort may vary significantly throughout production. Some areas may require only lightweight revisions, while others may evolve into deeper UX exploration, validation, prototyping, and refinement cycles depending on workflow complexity, feedback, and operational findings discovered during the project.
The provided time estimations represent reasonable projections based on the currently available information and product structure at the time of evaluation.

name: 5.5.2.A.2 - Service Detail Page Template Layout Design
text_content:
Design and refinement of individual service-detail pages used to present specific services, deliverables, use cases, and supporting informational content.
The current detail pages appear highly repetitive and visually limited despite representing highly important operational and commercial offerings of the platform. 

During the redesign process, these pages may evolve into richer presentation experiences supported by stronger layout structures, contextual visuals, production examples, testimonials, previous project references, or supporting media depending on the selected design direction.
Particular attention may be given to helping users more quickly understand:
what the service provides,
when it should be used,
what deliverables are included,
and why the service may be valuable for their specific workflow or property type.

If it would not create any marketing or operational concerns for your side, pricing information related to each service should ideally also be accessible directly within the corresponding service pages in order to provide clearer expectations and support more informed decision-making throughout the user journey.

The goal will be creating a clearer, more premium, and more confidence-building service presentation experience throughout the platform.

name: 5.4.1 - New Order Selection Main Screen
text_content:
During the estimation and structural evaluation process, it became increasingly clear that the current order creation experience would benefit from a more functionally separated and user-friendly grouping structure. For this reason, the redesigned Create New Order experience will likely present workflows under two primary categories: creating new production orders from scratch, and working with existing property-related assets already available within the FPV ecosystem.

This approach aims to reduce complexity, improve clarity, and help users navigate operationally dense workflows in a more intuitive and manageable way. Additionally, when users begin creating a completely new order, the system may also benefit from informing or warning them if an existing plan, survey, or related property asset already exists within the inventory system based on the provided property information.

text_content:
This area represents one of the most critical parts of the overall product experience. The order creation process directly affects usability, operational clarity, user confidence, and the overall perception of the platform.

My primary focus during the redesign of this area will be helping users complete what is currently a highly step-heavy and operationally dense workflow in a clearer, more comfortable, and more manageable way.

Since these flows represent core operational services of the platform, they may require additional UX planning, new screen structures, interaction patterns, and dedicated workflow solutions throughout the redesign process.

Due to the complexity and operational importance of this area, certain sections may require additional UX exploration, alternative structural solutions, iterative testing, and multiple refinement cycles in order to achieve the most effective workflow structure. The answers to the questions that will be asked throughout this process, along with additional notes and feedback from both you and your customers, will be especially critical for this area.

name: 5.3.3 - AutoFill Preferences
text_content:
Although this area may initially appear as a simple settings or form screen, it actually represents a highly operational and workflow-critical preference system directly connected to future order creation flows.

The current structure contains a large amount of configuration-heavy content, repeated operational decisions, deliverable preferences, formatting logic, conditional options, and production-related defaults that may significantly affect the usability and efficiency of future order workflows.

Because of this, the redesign process for this area will likely require careful UX simplification, content grouping, hierarchy restructuring, progressive disclosure solutions, and improved interaction patterns in order to reduce cognitive load and create a more manageable and user-friendly experience.

Particular attention may also be required for:
content density reduction
smarter grouping structures
reusable preference logic
expandable sections
onboarding clarity
conditional visibility patterns
repeat-order usability improvements

To improve workflow efficiency and reduce repetitive manual input, larger form-based and operational data-entry areas throughout the app may also benefit from contextual “Save as AutoFill Preference” actions. This would allow users — particularly real estate professionals handling repeated order workflows — to quickly reuse previously entered information, preferences, and operational settings across future orders and related production flows.

The overall structure and operational behavior of this area may continue evolving throughout the redesign process depending on workflow findings and real usage requirements.

name: Full App Design
text_content:
Full UI design process covering the existing FPV mobile app structure, including screens, sub-pages, modals, forms, and main and sub-navigation components. The current app structure is used as a reference foundation during the redesign process; however, layouts, navigation systems, page structures, flows, and screen organization may continue evolving throughout the UX analysis, solution development, and design production phases.

The subtasks and estimations listed throughout this proposal are primarily based on the current app structure and available screens at the time of evaluation. Depending on product and usability needs, new screens may be introduced, existing screens may be reorganized or divided into multiple parts, and interface structures may be refined to create a more scalable, functional, and improved user experience.

This phase will progress screen-by-screen throughout the production process. The workflow will generally include reviewing the current experience, identifying UX opportunities and structural problems, developing UX and interface solutions, producing updated UI designs, sharing the results for review, collecting revisions and feedback directly within Figma through pin-based comments and discussions, and iteratively refining the designs until satisfying results are achieved.

As the redesign evolves, many areas of the product may continue changing organically through ongoing UX evaluations, design decisions, workflow discoveries, usability considerations, and iterative refinement cycles during production.

name: 4.1 - Wordmark & Emblem Rework [IMPORTANT]
text_content:
This topic is highly important and I will need your guidance and thoughts regarding the brand direction throughout this process.

We need to refine the existing wordmark to achieve a cleaner, more contemporary, and stronger visual identity. The current approach focuses on improving the overall visual quality through typography refinement, balanced proportions, and stronger brand presence rather than conducting a deep full-scale branding process.

Most importantly, the brand currently appears to rely primarily on the existing wordmark, and I was unable to identify an actively used emblem across either the website or the app experience. If there is a specific reason behind this, I would be glad to understand the background and current brand considerations.

From a product and mobile app perspective, I believe the platform would strongly benefit from having a more recognizable brand emblem. A refined or newly developed emblem direction could help establish a more cohesive visual identity across the mobile app, website, and future product ecosystem.

Depending on the importance level of this topic for your brand, I would actually recommend treating this as a more dedicated and foundational design process early in the project timeline. The character, typography, and visual personality of the wordmark and emblem directly influence the overall UI language and design direction that should be built around the product.

At the same time, I understand that you may not currently want to approach this as a separate standalone rebranding project. Because of that, the current task is planned primarily as a refinement and modernization process based on the existing visual materials and overall brand direction.

Rather than being handled as a completely isolated production stage, many of these design system decisions and refinements will continue evolving organically throughout the UI design process as the product language, interface needs, and overall visual direction become clearer.

An important part of this phase is directly connected to the overall branding foundation of the product. If there is an existing and actively usable branding direction, visual identity system, or brand guidelines, these tasks will be shaped and supported accordingly. Otherwise, parts of this phase may naturally evolve into a lighter branding-oriented process while the product’s visual language and interface identity are being established. I will also need guidance and additional information from your side regarding the current branding direction, existing assets, and long-term brand expectations throughout this process.

name: Overall UX Analysis & Screen Solutions
text_content:

This phase represents the continuous UX-focused layer of the project rather than a separate one-time analysis stage. The goal is to progressively improve usability, clarity, layout structure, navigation behavior, interaction quality, and overall interface consistency as the product design evolves.

Throughout the design process, I will also be asking screen-specific and workflow-specific questions regarding the core purpose of pages, component functionality, user expectations, operational requirements, and existing product problems. These discussions will play a critical role in restructuring screens, improving UX decisions, and building more effective interface solutions during the redesign process.

Due to the iterative and ongoing nature of these UX-focused processes throughout the redesign workflow, estimation is handled collectively at the phase level rather than as isolated screen tasks.
